whatCounterClaimIsParagraph1: cyA counterclaim is a separate claim that you can make alongside your response. You can make it against:
whatCounterClaimIsBullet2: cya party and someone else that can be considered as part of the same case
whatCounterClaimIsParagraph2: cyIt's separate from the questions you've already answered, where you responded to specific information Treetops Housing provided. In a counterclaim, you say that a party owes you something. This can be money or something else.
whatCounterClaimIsParagraph3: cyYou can make a counterclaim at any time, but if you do it after you've submitted your response, you will have to ask a judge for permission to make it.
whatCounterClaimIsLinkText: cyRead guidance on counterclaims from Shelter (opens in new tab)
whenMakeCounterClaimHeading: cyWhen you can make a counterclaim
whenMakeCounterClaimParagraph1: cyYou can make a counterclaim if you believe a party has done something wrong or failed to act. For example, if they:
whenMakeCounterClaimBullet1: cyleft the property in a state of disrepair
whenMakeCounterClaimBullet2: cydid not protect your tenancy deposit
whenMakeCounterClaimParagraph2: cyYou must have a valid legal reason to make a counterclaim.
whenMakeCounterClaimParagraph3: cyIf you're not sure whether you have a valid reason, you can get free legal advice through the Housing Loss Prevention Advice Service.You can find a provider near you using the find a legal adviser service (opens in new tab).
counterClaimFeesParagraph1: cyYou will have to pay a fee to make a counterclaim, unless you're eligible for help with fees.Check if you can get help with court fees (opens in new tab)
counterClaimFeesParagraph2: cyThe amount you pay depends on what you’re claiming. If it’s a claim for an amount of money, the fee depends on the value of your claim. If your claim is about something else, the fee is {{counterClaimFlatFeeFEE0450, currency(currency: GBP; trailingZeroDisplay: stripIfInteger)}}.
counterClaimFeesCourtFeesLinkText: cyFind out about court fees (opens in new tab)
question: cyDo you want to make a counterclaim?

errors.makeCounterClaim: cySelect if you're planning to make a counterclaim
